metadata.description: Consolidated synbiotic mix reference — 8 mixes with strains, default prebiotic formulas, and clinical triggers.
metadata.note: LPc-37 (IFF) psychobiotic enhancement is applied separately by the pipeline (not baked into mixes). Mix 7 is the sole exception — LPc-37 is a core named strain in that mix at 10B. NOTE: LPc-37 replaced LP815 (Verb Biotics) on 2026-04-01 at Vitema's instruction. LP815 was the original psychobiotic strain; LPc-37 is the current IFF-sourced replacement. NOTE: B. infantis/longum 35624 (Novonesis) replaced by B. longum 1714 (Novonesis) in Mixes 2 and 5 on 2026-04-08 — 35624 confirmed no production by supplier.

1.clinical_purpose: Restore microbiota after severe depletion (post-antibiotic, very low abundance, severe dysbiosis)

ecological_rationale.scientific: Broad ecosystem dysfunction with ≥3 beneficial guilds depleted indicates systemic trophic chain collapse. Selective feeding of individual guilds is insufficient when cross-feeding networks are severed. This mix uses phylogenetically diverse strains (Bifidobacterium, Lactobacillus, Streptococcus) to re-seed multiple metabolic niches simultaneously, combined with mixed-fermentation prebiotics (inulin + PHGG + FOS) to provide substrate across the saccharolytic → acetogenic → butyrogenic chain.
ecological_rationale.client_friendly: Multiple groups of beneficial bacteria are depleted at once. Rather than trying to fix one at a time, this mix provides a broad foundation of diverse bacteria along with a mix of fiber types, giving the ecosystem the raw materials to rebuild itself naturally.

selection_scenarios.scientific: Multi-guild depletion exceeds single-target intervention capacity. Trophic chain is broken at multiple points — requires ecosystem-level reset with phylogenetically diverse inoculants.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: More than half of the beneficial bacteria groups are depleted. The gut needs a comprehensive restart rather than targeted support for one specific group.

selection_scenarios.condition: Fiber degraders below range + negative CLR (<-0.3)
selection_scenarios.scientific: Negative CLR indicates fiber degraders are losing competitive fitness — they're being outcompeted for ecological niches. Substrate supplementation alone won't work because competitors will intercept the substrates. Broad-spectrum ecosystem reset is needed to reduce competitive pressure before fiber-specific support can succeed.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: The fiber-digesting bacteria aren't just low — they're actively losing ground to other bacteria. Simply providing more fiber won't help because other organisms will consume it first. A broader ecosystem reset is needed to rebalance the playing field.

selection_scenarios.scientific: Cross-feeders (acetogenic bridge organisms) connect the fiber degradation and butyrate production pathways. When this bridge guild is depleted, the entire SCFA trophic chain is severed regardless of upstream/downstream guild health. Ecosystem-level restoration is required to rebuild the metabolic linkages.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: Cross-feeding bacteria act as a bridge between different bacterial groups. When this bridge is broken, nutrients can't flow properly through the ecosystem — a broad restoration is needed to rebuild these connections.

strains.name: Lactobacillus plantarum 299v
strains.role: SCFA protection, IBS support, competitive exclusion anchor

strains.name: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG
strains.role: Epithelial barrier protection, AAD/CDAD prevention, pathogen exclusion

strains.name: Lactobacillus acidophilus NCFM
strains.role: Antimicrobial compound production, pathogen exclusion, lactose digestion

strains.name: Bifidobacterium longum Bl-05
strains.role: Acetate producer, cross-feeding hub for guild restoration

strains.name: Lactobacillus reuteri UALre-16
strains.role: Reuterin (antimicrobial) production, histamine-mediated immune modulation

components.rationale: Gentle distal fermentation base — tolerated even in sensitive gut
Butyrate substrate; broad saccharolytic/acetogenic chain support
Must-include — minimum effective dose for trophic chain signalling
Must-include — minimum dose for Bifidogenic cross-feeding
Must-include — minimum dose for saccharolytic substrate diversity

default_prebiotic_formula.rationale: Offline gentle-first formula: PHGG + Beta-glucans carry bulk weight (low gas, non-FODMAP). All must_include substrates (Inulin, GOS, FOS, Beta-glucans) present at minimum effective doses. Treats all offline clients as sensitive.

2.clinical_purpose: Selectively enrich Bifidobacterium when depleted (IBS, constipation, low Bifido guild)

ecological_rationale.scientific: Selective Bifidobacterium depletion without broader dysbiosis indicates a guild-specific deficit in the acetogenic/lactate-producing niche. Bifidobacteria are keystone organisms for lactate cross-feeding, immune modulation (Treg induction), and barrier-protective signaling. This mix uses 3 complementary Bifidobacterium species (B. infantis/longum, B. animalis lactis B420, B. animalis lactis BB-12, B. breve BR03+B632) alongside Bifido-selective prebiotics (GOS, Lactulose, Inulin) to selectively enrich this guild without disturbing other functional groups.
ecological_rationale.client_friendly: One specific group of beneficial bacteria — Bifidobacteria — is depleted while the rest of the gut ecosystem is healthy. This mix focuses specifically on rebuilding this important group using targeted probiotic strains and prebiotics that preferentially feed Bifidobacteria.

selection_scenarios.scientific: Guild-specific depletion of Bifidobacterium spp. with preserved broader ecosystem integrity. Selective Bifido restoration via conspecific strains + highly Bifido-selective oligosaccharides (GOS, lactulose) avoids unnecessary perturbation of stable guilds.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: Bifidobacteria levels are low but other bacterial groups are fine. A targeted approach restores this specific group without disrupting the rest of the gut balance.

strains.name: Bifidobacterium longum 1714
strains.role: Primary IBS anchor and Bifido restoration, IL-10/IL-12 normalisation
strains.replacement_reason: 35624 confirmed no production by Novonesis (Apr 2026). B. longum 1714 selected as replacement per supplier availability.

strains.name: Bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis B420
strains.role: Metabolic Bifido — acetate/propionate production, GPR43-PPARα signalling

strains.name: Bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis BB-12
strains.role: Constipation relief, bile salt hydrolase activity, mucus adherence, pathogen inhibition

strains.name: Lactobacillus acidophilus NCFM
strains.role: Lactate producer feeding Bifidobacteria via cross-feeding; IBS support, gas reduction

strains.name: Bifidobacterium breve BR03 + Bifidobacterium breve B632 (premix)
strains.role: Serpin-producing capacity — serine protease inhibition reducing IBS-associated proteolytic activity

components.rationale: Gentle base — tolerated in sensitive gut, slow distal fermentation
Must-include — primary Bifido-selective oligosaccharide; minimum effective dose
Must-include — NCFM degrades oligofructose releasing lactate for Bifido cross-feeding
Must-include — short-chain Bifido substrate diversity

default_prebiotic_formula.rationale: Offline gentle-first formula: PHGG carries bulk weight (non-FODMAP). GOS + Inulin + FOS all present as must_include at minimum doses. Lactulose omitted (highly_recommended only) — too osmotically aggressive for sensitive clients in offline mode.

3.clinical_purpose: Restore SCFA-producing guild when fiber fermenters / butyrate producers are low

ecological_rationale.scientific: Restores the complete SCFA trophic chain: fiber degradation → acetate/lactate production → butyrate conversion. Uses Lactobacillus plantarum UALp-05™ + 299v (mechanistic cross-feeders for Clostridiales butyrate producers) + L. acidophilus NCFM (degrades inulin-type fructans releasing fructose and producing lactate that Anaerostipes caccae and F. prausnitzii convert to butyrate) + BB-12 (acetate donor) + complex polysaccharide prebiotics (Beta-Glucans, PHGG) that preferentially feed Bacteroidetes/Firmicutes fiber degraders. The therapeutic endpoint is restored butyrate production — the primary fuel for colonocytes and barrier integrity.
ecological_rationale.client_friendly: Your fiber-processing bacteria need reinforcement — they are below the healthy range. This mix restores the complete chain from fiber digestion to short-chain fatty acid (SCFA) production, which feeds your gut lining and reduces inflammation. The prebiotic fiber blend in your sachet (PHGG, Beta-Glucans) works together with these probiotics — a dual intervention targeting the same guild from both the bacterial and substrate sides.

selection_scenarios.scientific: Direct butyrogenic guild depletion — insufficient Faecalibacterium/Roseburia abundance for adequate colonocyte fuel supply. Cross-feeding Lactobacillus strains provide lactate/acetate substrates that Clostridiales convert to butyrate.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: Butyrate-producing bacteria are low. This mix provides helper bacteria that produce the raw materials butyrate producers need, along with specialized fiber to fuel the whole process.

selection_scenarios.condition: Fiber degraders below range + positive CLR (>+0.3)
selection_scenarios.scientific: Positive CLR = fiber degraders are competitively fit (winning their niche) but substrate-limited. The guild has the organisms — it lacks the food. Complex polysaccharide supplementation (Beta-Glucans, PHGG, Inulin) directly addresses the bottleneck. Butyrate strains ensure downstream conversion is ready when upstream expansion occurs.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: The fiber-digesting bacteria are capable and growing — they just don't have enough of the right food. This mix provides the specific types of fiber they prefer, along with support bacteria to ensure the whole digestion chain works efficiently.

selection_scenarios.condition: Fiber degraders below range + neutral CLR (between -0.3 and +0.3)
selection_scenarios.scientific: Neutral CLR is indeterminate — no clear competitive advantage or disadvantage. Default to substrate support (Mix 3) rather than ecosystem reset (Mix 1), as substrate limitation is the more common and less invasive explanation.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: The fiber-digesting bacteria are low, but there's no clear sign they're being outcompeted. We provide additional fiber substrate as the most likely and gentlest solution.

strains.name: Lactobacillus plantarum UALp-05
strains.role: High-output saccharolytic fermenter; lactate and acetate cross-feeding substrate for Clostridiales butyrate producers

strains.name: Lactobacillus plantarum 299v
strains.role: Evidence-based IBS support; maintains total SCFA during antibiotic challenge; redundant cross-feeding strain

strains.name: Lactobacillus acidophilus NCFM
strains.role: Degrades inulin-type fructans (FOS/oligofructose) releasing free fructose and lactate as substrate for butyrate producers

strains.name: Bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis BB-12
strains.role: Acetate producer feeding butyrate producers; bifidobacterial supplementation increases acetate and modifies colonic fermentation

components.rationale: Primary butyrate substrate — best match for dual-plantarum cross-feeding; non-FODMAP
Gentle distal fermentation base — tolerated in sensitive gut
Must-include — NCFM degrades fructans releasing lactate for butyrate producers
Must-include (v2) — minimum dose for NCFM oligofructose signalling
Must-include — short-chain substrate diversity for SCFA trophic chain

default_prebiotic_formula.rationale: Offline gentle-first formula: Beta-glucans + PHGG carry 5/7g (non-FODMAP). Must-include substrates (Inulin, FOS, GOS, Beta-glucans) all present at minimum effective doses. Treats all offline clients as sensitive.

gassy_client_formula.rationale: 3:1:4 ratio heavily weights PHGG for gas-prone clients. Minimal inulin retained for NCFM fructan-degradation signalling.

4.clinical_purpose: Suppress proteolytic/pathobiont overgrowth (malodorous stool, high proteolytic markers, food sensitivities)

ecological_rationale.scientific: Elevated proteolytic guild (Bilophila, Desulfovibrio, Clostridium clusters) indicates excessive protein fermentation producing hydrogen sulfide, ammonia, p-cresol, and indole — cytotoxic metabolites that damage barrier integrity and promote inflammation. Intervention strategy: competitive exclusion via saccharolytic strains (L. plantarum 299v, B. longum Bl-05) that shift fermentation balance from proteolytic → saccharolytic pathways. L. reuteri UALre-16 produces reuterin with direct antimicrobial activity against proteolytic pathogens. L. casei 431 supports sIgA production to control pathobiont populations. Psyllium binds endotoxin; Beta-Glucans provide alternative carbohydrate substrate.
ecological_rationale.client_friendly: Certain bacteria that break down protein into harmful byproducts have become overgrown. This mix introduces beneficial bacteria that compete with these harmful organisms for resources, while providing plant-based fiber that shifts the gut's chemistry away from protein breakdown toward healthier carbohydrate digestion.

selection_scenarios.scientific: Proteolytic overgrowth produces cytotoxic metabolites (H₂S, p-cresol, ammonia) via amino acid fermentation. Competitive exclusion strategy: flood the environment with saccharolytic organisms and carbohydrate substrates to outcompete protein fermenters for metabolic niche space.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: Bacteria that produce harmful chemicals from protein digestion have become overgrown. This mix crowds them out with beneficial bacteria that prefer to digest plant-based fibers instead.

strains.name: Lactobacillus plantarum 299v
strains.role: Saccharolytic competition, pH acidification via lactate, competitive displacement of proteolytic bacteria

strains.name: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG
strains.role: Barrier protection against pathobiont translocation; metabolises mucin-derived sugars

strains.name: Bifidobacterium longum Bl-05
strains.role: Acetate production lowers luminal pH, inhibiting proteolytic Clostridia and Bacteroides

strains.name: Lactobacillus reuteri UALre-16
strains.role: Reuterin (3-HPA) antimicrobial production — direct activity against proteolytic pathogens

strains.name: Lactobacillus casei 431
strains.role: Immune modulation; sIgA production to help control pathobiont populations

components.rationale: Primary carbohydrate substrate — outcompetes proteolytic bacteria for fermentation niche
Must-include (highly_recommended) — binds endotoxin and proteolytic metabolites; non-FODMAP
Gentle base fiber for sensitive gut; distal fermentation
Must-include — fermentable carbohydrate for saccharolytic competitive displacement
Must-include — minimum dose for Bifido substrate diversity
Must-include — short-chain carbohydrate substrate for competitive niche flooding

default_prebiotic_formula.rationale: Offline gentle-first formula: Beta-glucans + Psyllium + PHGG carry 5/7g (non-FODMAP). Psyllium is clinically important for endotoxin binding in proteolytic suppression. Must-include FODMAP substrates (Inulin, GOS, FOS) present at minimum effective doses.

5.clinical_purpose: Restore mucus barrier and support Akkermansia axis (leaky gut, zonulin↑, barrier dysfunction)

ecological_rationale.scientific: Elevated mucin-degrading organisms (primarily Akkermansia muciniphila) with diet-fed metabolism (MDR <-0.5 or neutral) indicates barrier dysfunction rather than competitive displacement. The mucin degraders are receiving substrate from dietary sources or have indeterminate metabolic patterns. Barrier-protective strains restore tight junction signaling: L. plantarum 299v stimulates MUC2/MUC3 goblet cell secretion; BB-12 and HN019 have strong mucus adherence; B. infantis/longum 35624 provides serpin-like anti-inflammatory barrier protection; B. breve BR03+B632 directly inhibit serine proteases that degrade mucus. Inulin + PHGG support SCFA-driven barrier repair without feeding Akkermansia overgrowth.
ecological_rationale.client_friendly: Bacteria that thin the gut's protective mucus layer have become overgrown, potentially weakening the gut barrier. This mix provides bacteria that help strengthen and rebuild the gut lining, along with gentle fibers that support healing without feeding the problematic organisms.

selection_scenarios.scientific: Negative MDR indicates mucin degraders are diet-fed (not mucus-dependent) — barrier stress is occurring through dietary substrate rather than direct mucus consumption. Barrier-protective strains restore tight junction integrity; prebiotics support SCFA-driven epithelial repair.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: Mucus-thinning bacteria are overgrown and being fed by the diet. Barrier-strengthening bacteria and gentle fiber support help rebuild gut lining protection.

selection_scenarios.condition: Mucin degraders above range + neutral MDR (between -0.5 and +0.5)
selection_scenarios.scientific: Indeterminate mucin metabolism pattern — default to barrier restoration (safer approach) rather than competitive displacement. Conservative strategy avoids aggressive niche competition when metabolic context is unclear.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: Mucus-thinning bacteria are elevated. We focus on strengthening the gut barrier as the safest and most effective response.

strains.name: Lactobacillus plantarum 299v
strains.role: Stimulates MUC2/MUC3 mucin secretion from goblet cells; lactic acid and bacteriocins prevent pathogen attachment

strains.name: Bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis BB-12
strains.role: Strong mucus adherence; SCFA-driven tight-junction strengthening; barrier function enhancement

strains.name: Bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis HN019
strains.role: Strong mucus adherence; mucosal immune modulation; pathogen inhibition at mucosal surface

strains.name: Bifidobacterium longum 1714
strains.role: Serpin-like activity reduces protease-mediated barrier degradation; anti-inflammatory IL-10 induction protects mucosa
strains.replacement_reason: 35624 confirmed no production by Novonesis (Apr 2026). B. longum 1714 selected as replacement per supplier availability.

strains.name: Bifidobacterium breve BR03 + Bifidobacterium breve B632 (premix)
strains.role: Serpin-positive strains — directly inhibit serine proteases that degrade mucus layer; EPS production forms protective biofilm-like layer

low_akkermansia_formula.rationale: Polyphenol-driven Akkermansia enrichment + SCFA barrier support.

high_akkermansia_formula.rationale: Avoid feeding Akkermansia. Curcumin/quercetin support barrier independently.

components.rationale: Gentle barrier-repair base — SCFA-driven epithelial support without gas risk
SCFA substrate for tight-junction repair; non-FODMAP
Must-include (v2) — preferred substrate for Bifido-heavy mix (BB-12, HN019, 1714, BR03+B632)
Must-include — minimum dose for SCFA trophic chain signalling
Must-include — short-chain substrate diversity

default_prebiotic_formula.rationale: Offline gentle-first formula: PHGG + Beta-glucans carry 3.5/5.5g (non-FODMAP). GOS prioritised as must-include (v2 Bifido-heavy composition). Must-include FODMAP substrates at minimum effective doses. Treats all offline clients as sensitive.

6.clinical_purpose: Sustain eubiosis in healthy or recovering gut; chronic GI support

ecological_rationale.scientific: All functional guilds at Monitor priority — no critical ecological imbalances detected. 6-strain formulation (4 Lactobacillus/Streptococcus spp., 2 Bifidobacterium entries) maintains ecosystem stability via phylogenetic breadth. S. thermophilus UASt-09™ is unique — lactase-positive, operates in the small intestine, produces folate, formate, and thermophilin. Minimal prebiotic load (4g) avoids unnecessary perturbation of a balanced ecosystem; client dietary fiber provides the majority of substrate.
ecological_rationale.client_friendly: The gut ecosystem is well-balanced — no major imbalances were detected. This maintenance formula provides a broad spectrum of beneficial bacteria to preserve this healthy state, with a gentle amount of fiber to complement an already healthy diet.

selection_scenarios.condition: All guilds at Monitor priority — no actionable imbalances
selection_scenarios.scientific: No actionable guild imbalances. Maintenance-level intervention preserves ecosystem resilience without risking perturbation of stable community structures. Low prebiotic dose (4g) supplements but does not override dietary fiber intake.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: Everything looks good! This gentle maintenance formula keeps the gut balanced while the body's own diet provides most of the beneficial fiber.

strains.role: CLA production, lactose metabolism, general saccharolytic activity

Must-include substrates (GOS, Inulin, FOS, Beta-glucans) present at minimum doses. Treats all offline clients as sensitive.

7.clinical_purpose: Support gut-brain axis in dysbiosis with mood/anxiety/stress component (adjunct, not standalone)

strains.name: Bifidobacterium longum 04 + Lactobacillus fermentum LF16 + Lactobacillus plantarum LP01 + Lactobacillus rhamnosus LR06 (premix)
strains.role: Psychobiotic 4-strain premix — GABA production, HPA axis modulation, serotonin-pathway support, gut-brain axis signalling bridge. L. rhamnosus LR06 induces region-dependent GABA receptor expression changes; B. longum 04 modulates brain activity during social stress.

strains.name: Lactobacillus paracasei LPC-37
strains.role: Psychobiotic strain — gut-brain axis support; core strain always included in Mix 7. REPLACEMENT NOTE (2026-04-01): This strain replaced LP815 (Lactiplantibacillus plantarum, Verb Biotics) per Vitema Pharmaceuticals instruction. LP815 was the original high-potency GABA producer

components.rationale: Gentle base — bulk fermentation load; non-FODMAP; well tolerated in stress-gut clients
Must-include — SCFA substrate supporting GABA precursor environment
Must-include — specifically required for L. fermentum LF16 GABA production pathway
Must-include — minimum Bifido substrate; B. longum 04 cross-feeding support
Must-include — short-chain serotonin-pathway substrate diversity

default_prebiotic_formula.rationale: Offline gentle-first formula: PHGG + RS carry 3/5g (non-FODMAP). RS is clinically obligatory for LF16 GABA precursor environment. Must-include FODMAP substrates (Inulin, GOS, FOS) present at minimum doses. Treats all offline clients as sensitive.

fodmap_sensitive_formula.rationale: Reduced FODMAP for sensitive clients while maintaining polyphenol neuroprotection. Resistant starch substrate maintained via beta-glucans if available.

8.clinical_purpose: Competitive displacement of Akkermansia overgrowth via Fiber guild expansion when MDR >+0.5

ecological_rationale.scientific: Akkermansia overgrowth (>10%) with positive MDR (>+0.5) and fiber degrader depletion indicates mucus-dependent metabolism — Akkermansia is consuming the mucus layer because the Fiber guild can't provide sufficient carbohydrate competition. Strategy: competitive displacement via niche expansion, NOT direct suppression. Complex polysaccharides (Beta-Glucans, Psyllium/Arabinoxylan, Resistant Starch) that Bacteroidetes/Firmicutes excel at but Akkermansia doesn't preferentially use. L. plantarum UALp-05™ and 299v provide dual saccharolytic dominance. As fiber guild expands, Akkermansia pressure naturally reduces through substrate competition.
ecological_rationale.client_friendly: A specific bacterium (Akkermansia) has overgrown because the fiber-digesting bacteria aren't providing enough competition. Rather than trying to kill the overgrown organism, this mix feeds and strengthens the fiber-digesting bacteria so they naturally crowd out the problematic one — like growing grass to crowd out weeds, rather than using herbicide.

selection_scenarios.condition: Mucin degraders above range + MDR >+0.5 (mucus-dependent) + Fiber degraders below range
selection_scenarios.scientific: Mucus-dependent Akkermansia metabolism (MDR >+0.5) with concurrent fiber guild substrate bottleneck. Root cause = insufficient carbohydrate competition forces Akkermansia to rely on mucus as primary carbon source. Competitive displacement via complex polysaccharide loading (Beta-Glucans, RS, Arabinoxylan) expands fiber guild niche, reducing Akkermansia's ecological advantage.
selection_scenarios.client_friendly: Akkermansia is consuming the gut's protective mucus because there aren't enough fiber-digesting bacteria to compete with it. By boosting the fiber bacteria with their preferred food, they naturally push Akkermansia back to healthy levels — addressing the root cause, not just the symptom.

strains.name: Bifidobacterium animalis subsp. lactis BB-12
strains.role: Acetate production for cross-feeding; mucus adherence blocks pathogen colonisation sites

strains.name: Bifidobacterium longum Bl-05

strains.name: Lactobacillus plantarum UALp-05
strains.role: Fiber fermentation specialist; dual plantarum strategy for maximum saccharolytic dominance and niche competitive pressure

8.mechanism: Competitive displacement via Fiber guild expansion rather than direct Akkermansia suppression. Addresses root cause (substrate insufficiency) not symptom (Akkermansia overgrowth).

components.rationale: Primary Bacteroidetes/Firmicutes substrate
Complex polysaccharide Akk doesn't prefer
Slow fermentation, Fiber guild preferred
Gentle base fiber
Moderate dose for Bifido signalling only
Trace only — minimize Akk-accessible substrate

default_prebiotic_formula.rationale: HIGH LOAD complex polysaccharides (beta-glucans, arabinoxylan, RS) that Bacteroidetes/Firmicutes excel at but Akkermansia doesn't preferentially use. Creates competitive displacement via niche expansion.

akkermansia_safe_notes: AVOID high GOS (>1g) — accessible to Akkermansia
AVOID high FOS — simple oligosaccharides Akk can use
PREFER Beta-glucans, Psyllium, RS, PHGG — complex polysaccharides
Keep Inulin moderate (1-1.5g max) for Bifido signalling only

lpc37_enhancement.mechanism: Applied as cross-mix psychobiotic enhancement for stress/mood/sleep support. 5B CFU dose for Mixes 1-6 and 8; 10B as core strain in Mix 7.
lpc37_enhancement.replacement_note: LPc-37 replaced LP815 (Lactiplantibacillus plantarum, Verb Biotics) on 2026-04-01 per Vitema Pharmaceuticals instruction.
lpc37_enhancement.note: Mix 7 includes LPc-37 at 10B as a named core strain — the enhancement block does NOT add additional LPc-37 on top of Mix 7. For all other mixes, this enhancement adds 5B LPc-37 when stress/mood triggers are met.
trigger_rules: Stress ≥ 4/10 AND mood/anxiety is a stated goal → always add
lpc37_enhancement.prebiotic_impact: Add 0.5-1.0g Resistant Starch for psychobiotic substrate support
lpc37_enhancement.applied_to: Mixes 1-6, 8 only — enhancement, not replacement. Mix 7 has LPc-37 built in at 10B.
